```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))
## Parametros
theta <- sample(c(2, 3, 4, 5), 1)
n     <- sample(c(25, 50, 100), 1)

## g(theta) = theta^2, g'(theta) = 2*theta
## I(theta) = 1/theta^2 (Exponencial)
## CR bound para g(theta): [g'(theta)]^2 / (n*I(theta)) = (2*theta)^2 * theta^2 / n = 4*theta^4/n
correct <- round(4 * theta^4 / n, 4)
w1 <- round(4 * theta^2 / n, 4)
w2 <- round(4 * theta^4 * n, 4)
w3 <- round(2 * theta^4 / n, 4)
w4 <- round(4 * theta^4 / (n * theta^2), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
O **Limite de Cramer-Rao generalizado** para estimadores nao-viesados de $g(\theta)$ e:
$$\text{LICR}(g(\theta)) = \frac{[g'(\theta)]^2}{n\,I(\theta)}.$$

Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Exp}(\theta)$ com $I(\theta) = 1/\theta^2$, considere $g(\theta) = \theta^2$ (de modo que $g'(\theta) = 2\theta$).

Com $\theta = `r theta`$ e $n = `r n`$, qual e o LICR para estimadores nao-viesados de $g(\theta) = \theta^2$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Aplicando a formula:
$$\text{LICR}(g(\theta)) = \frac{[g'(\theta)]^2}{n\,I(\theta)} = \frac{(2\theta)^2}{n \cdot (1/\theta^2)} = \frac{4\theta^2 \cdot \theta^2}{n} = \frac{4\theta^4}{n}.$$

Para $\theta = `r theta`$ e $n = `r n`$:
$$\text{LICR} = \frac{4 \times `r theta`^4}{`r n`} = \frac{4 \times `r theta^4`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap4S2Q10_CR_funcao_parametro
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
